programs.title: Capturing Home
programs.desc: Their kids finally moved out, and a couple is ready to reclaim their home; they enlist Kate and Amy to help transform their dated family house into a functional, modern dwelling where they can entertain and relish their retirement.

programs.title: Building Off the Grid
programs.desc: Bamboo-home architect David Sands seizes the opportunity to build his own getaway in Hawaii that features a luxurious outdoor bath area and detached, starlit master bedroom; however, volcanic eruptions threaten to send the entire project up in smoke.

programs.title: Building Off the Grid
programs.desc: A family builds thousands of earth blocks while withstanding winter monsoons, freezing temperatures and more to finish their forever home in San Antonio, Texas.

programs.title: Building Off the Grid
programs.desc: Marine scientists in Florida hire engineers Nicolas Derouin and Arnaud Luguet to create a first-of-its-kind floating home; they have nine months to complete it, but unpredictable weather and building hiccups may sink this project before it can swim.

programs.title: Building Off the Grid
programs.desc: A couple plans to build an oceanfront oasis for their family in Maui, Hawaii and, to keep the budget low, they design a Quonset hut which can be built cheaply and quickly, but some setbacks test their limits at every turn.

programs.title: Building Outside the Lines
programs.sub_title: Historic Granary Becomes a Goat Milk Goods Haven
programs.desc: Cappie and Alex relocate and restore a 120-year-old granary, transforming it into a rustic-yet-functional retail space for goat milk-based products -- honoring their clients' expanding farm with personal touches and plenty of space to grow.
